typography.font_family: System (Roboto on Android, San Francisco on iOS)

primary_cta_fab.style: Large floating action button centered at the bottom or prominent on screen. Minimum 64x64px.

annotation_toolbar.style: Horizontal scrollable or grid tool palette overlay.
annotation_toolbar.position: bottom anchored above standard bottom sheet

color_swatch_picker.style: Horizontal row of circular color buttons.
color_swatch_picker.shadow: subtle drop shadow for visibility on images

bottom_sheet.style: Standard MD3 modal bottom sheet. Used for settings, export options, or tool expansion.

list_cards.style: Flat cards with subtle 1px border. No heavy drop shadows to keep UI fast and clean.

gallery_thumbnail_grid.overlay: Small observation number badge (e.g. OBS-0001) at bottom left of thumbnail, background: rgba(0,0,0,0.6)

gps_location_stamp_overlay.position: Bottom right or bottom left of the captured image.

watermark.position: Top right or center with low opacity.

images.description: Electrical switchboard, used to test annotation and gallery grid.
Complex electrical wiring for testing detailed drawings.
Intricate network of stainless steel pipes.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use React Native components or React Native Paper for Material Design 3 UI compliance. Develop using .js files rather than .tsx files.
Prioritize application speed: minimize animations, avoid complex nested views where possible.
Ensure ALL interactive elements (buttons, inputs, toggles, gallery items) have a minimum touch target size of 48x48px.
Implement `data-testid` on all interactive UI components, e.g., `data-testid="capture-photo-button"`, `data-testid="annotation-tool-circle"`.
Annotation Layer: Keep the annotation state separate from the image state until the user explicitly saves/exports.
Offline-First: If the network is unavailable for reverse geocoding, save the raw lat/long and queue a background task to resolve the address once reconnected.
Use the provided `annotation_palette` colors exactly as specified to ensure high contrast against complex photo backgrounds.
The UI is purely functional. Do not add decorative shadows or generic stock photos. Use the industrial sample photos provided for gallery/mockups.
Implement light and dark mode respecting the device's system preference.
